title: Hell Is Other Robots

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, here to lay down some old, old, incredibly old school beats, the Beastie Boys!
jokes.punchline: The escalating repetition of 'old' emphasizes just how ancient they are in the future
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective escalation that establishes the show's temporal premise while getting a chuckle

jokes.text: They're busting mad rhymes with an 80% success rate. I believe that qualifies as ill. At least from a technical standpoint.
jokes.punchline: Reducing street slang to clinical percentages and qualifying 'ill' with scientific precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only the Professor would analyze hip-hop performance this way. The formal language contrasting with 'ill' is brilliant

jokes.text: Will you guys shut up? I'm trying to look cool.
jokes.setup: Fry wanting to appear cool at a concert
jokes.punchline: The inherent contradiction of announcing your attempt to look cool
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry - earnest but missing the point. The irony is obvious but fits the character

jokes.text: Tonight we're going to party like it's 1999... again.
jokes.setup: Reference to Prince's famous party anthem
jokes.punchline: The casual 'again' reminder that they lived through that millennium
jokes.explanation: Clever temporal humor that works on multiple levels - the reference plus the future setting

jokes.text: Hey, Bender. Hey, Fender.
jokes.punchline: The robot naming convention revealed through similar sounding names
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that establishes robot naming conventions efficiently

jokes.text: You still working at Jack in the Box?
jokes.punchline: Robot working at a restaurant chain a thousand years in the future
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about corporate continuity in the future

jokes.text: That was a thousand years ago. Now we got seven. Cool. Can I borrow the new ones? And a couple of blank tapes?
jokes.setup: Fry expressing fandom for their five albums
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking to borrow their new music plus blank tapes for copying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry behavior - enthusiastic fan who immediately reveals he's a music pirate

jokes.text: Hey, Bender, why don't we ditch these organ sacks and hit the real party?
jokes.punchline: 'Organ sacks' as robot slang for humans
jokes.explanation: Brilliant robot slang that's both descriptively accurate and delightfully insulting

jokes.text: Hey, what kind of party is this? There's no booze and only one hooker.
jokes.setup: Bender arriving at what he expected to be a wild party
jokes.punchline: His disappointment measured by specific debauchery metrics
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - measuring party quality by vice availability. Good character consistency

jokes.text: Uh, hey, I'm no square... but isn't that counter-indicated by my operations manual?
jokes.punchline: Trying to sound cool while citing technical documentation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender trying to be cool but his robot nature showing through

jokes.text: Well, if jacking on will make strangers think I'm cool... I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Peer pressure to try electrical abuse
jokes.punchline: Bender's honest admission that he only cares about strangers' opinions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - completely honest about his shallow motivations

jokes.text: Easy, baby, you don't want to get hooked on this stuff. Ah, no need to worry. I don't have an addictive personality.
jokes.punchline: Bender's confident denial while clearly already enjoying it
jokes.explanation: Classic addiction story trope but works well for establishing the arc

jokes.text: Shut up, square!
jokes.setup: Fry asking about the bad neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Bender's sudden personality shift and use of dated slang
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's personality change through outdated slang, effective character work

jokes.text: Hey, do I preach to you when you're lying stoned in the gutter? No. So beat it!
jokes.punchline: Bender's indignant response comparing his situation to being in a gutter
jokes.explanation: Good structure and the comparison works well, classic addict defensiveness

jokes.text: Who was that guy? Your mama. Now shut up and drag me to work.
jokes.punchline: Bender's childish 'your mama' comeback followed by demanding to be dragged
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's deteriorated state through juvenile behavior

jokes.text: Our electric bill is climbing faster than a green snake up a sugarcane.
jokes.punchline: Hermes' Caribbean-influenced colorful expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes character voice with his unique expressions

jokes.text: If I didn't know better, I'd almost think he was abusing electricity. Bender? No way. I definitely would have noticed something.
jokes.punchline: Fry's oblivious confidence despite obvious signs
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness, good character consistency

jokes.text: Bender, why are you spending so much time in the bathroom? Are you jacking on in there? No! Don't come in!
jokes.punchline: Direct drug confrontation met with obvious addict denial
jokes.explanation: Standard addiction story beat but necessary for the arc

jokes.text: I know big Vinnie said he was giving me the kiss of death... but I still think he was gay. Did he use his tongue? A little.
jokes.punchline: Fry misinterpreting a death threat as romantic interest, Bender's casual confirmation
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Fry naivety and Bender's matter-of-fact response

jokes.text: Come on, universe! You big, mostly empty wuss! Give me all the juice you got!
jokes.punchline: Bender taunting the universe itself for electricity, 'mostly empty' insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of his addiction and the 'mostly empty' insult to the universe is inspired

jokes.text: Bender, we didn't mind your drinking or your kleptomania or your pornography ring. In fact, that's why we loved you.
jokes.punchline: List of terrible behaviors they actually loved him for
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character insight - they love him BECAUSE of his vices

jokes.text: Just like when my friend Richie swore he wasn't taking drugs... and then he sold me my mom's VCR. And then later, I found out he was taking drugs.
jokes.punchline: The backwards revelation - he found out about drugs after the theft, missing the obvious connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - completely missing obvious cause and effect relationships

jokes.text: I see a lot of fancy robots here today... made of real shiny metal. But that don't impress the robot devil, no, sir.
jokes.punchline: Focusing on the shininess of metal as meaningless vanity
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific religious imagery that feels authentic to the world

jokes.text: he's going to plug his infernal modem in the wall... belching smoke and flame, and he's going to download you straight to robot hell.
jokes.punchline: Hell reimagined as a download process with modem connection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant technological reinterpretation of damnation that feels both funny and appropriately ominous

jokes.text: This is a witch hunt. Oh, what a beautiful morning, oh, what a beautiful day.
jokes.punchline: Bender's cheerful singing immediately after
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Hermes' complaint and Bender's inappropriate cheerfulness

jokes.text: Great. He's whacked out on electricity again. No, I'm whacked out on life.
jokes.punchline: Bender's earnest correction about being high on life
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of expectations with Bender's genuine enthusiasm for religion

jokes.text: Religion? Is this another scam to get free yarmulkes?
jokes.punchline: Fry's suspicion based on previous religious scamming
jokes.explanation: Good character history reference and implies Bender's past religious scams

jokes.text: Then you'll all come to my exceedingly long... un-air-conditioned baptism ceremony.
jokes.punchline: The specific uncomfortable details he emphasizes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender making his religious event sound as unappealing as possible

jokes.text: Tell it, preacher! That equals true.
jokes.punchline: Robot congregation using mathematical/logical affirmations
jokes.explanation: Clever robot take on church responses using their logical nature

jokes.text: Press any key to continue.
jokes.punchline: Treating baptism like software installation
jokes.explanation: Perfect computer/religion fusion joke with maximum economy and originality

jokes.text: Uh, while you're at it... could you touch up this seam?
jokes.punchline: Treating religious ceremony as practical maintenance opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - even during baptism, he's thinking practically about maintenance

jokes.text: No poison for us, thanks. I'll stick with good old mineral oil.
jokes.punchline: Bender calling alcohol poison and preferring robot lubricant
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing his religious conversion through practical robot terms

jokes.text: I'm hungrier than a green snake in a sugarcane field.
jokes.punchline: Using Hermes' earlier colorful expression incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Hermes' expression with Fry getting it slightly wrong

jokes.text: One-zero-zero-zero... one-zero-one-zero-one-zero-one-zero-one... zero-zero-one... zero-one-one-zero-zero-one... two.
jokes.punchline: Long binary sequence ending with regular number 'two'
jokes.explanation: Clever robot prayer concept with good payoff on the final 'two'

jokes.text: I'd like to give everyone hugs. Come here, Fry. Oh, uh, but I don't want to.
jokes.setup: Bender wanting to spread love through hugs
jokes.punchline: Fry's honest reluctance to be hugged
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy from the awkwardness of forced affection

jokes.text: What are you doing to my ship? Sanctifying it. There. That ought to convert a few tailgaters.
jokes.setup: Bender doing something to the ship
jokes.punchline: Using religious imagery to get converts from other drivers
jokes.explanation: Good pun on 'convert' and nice character moment of Bender's proselytizing

jokes.text: If only he had joined a mainstream religion... like Oprah-ism or voodoo.
jokes.punchline: Treating Oprah worship as more mainstream than Christianity/traditional religion
jokes.explanation: Clever commentary on pop culture worship and future religious landscape

jokes.text: Bender's stupid religion is driving me nuts. / Amen.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about Bender's religious phase
jokes.punchline: Leela responding with religious language while agreeing
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic humor with Leela unconsciously using religious language while complaining about religion.

jokes.text: But those girls don't wear cases. You can see their bare circuits.
jokes.punchline: Robot equivalent of nudity concern - seeing circuits instead of skin
jokes.explanation: Clever robot-specific take on modesty and nudity

jokes.text: Thou shalt not snatch.
jokes.punchline: Biblical commandment adapted for purse snatching
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-specific religious adaptation

jokes.text: I'm the greatest!
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate return to his egotistical self
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - immediate return to classic Bender

jokes.text: You know, as a major Hollywood director... I'll be holding auditions tonight for my next movie.
jokes.punchline: Using the classic sleazy pickup line
jokes.explanation: Standard sleazy pickup line, works for character but not very original

jokes.text: What in hell happened to Bender? Well, he didn't check out. The ashtray's still here.
jokes.punchline: Fry's literal interpretation of hotel checkout procedure
jokes.explanation: Good Fry logic - missing the obvious while focusing on mundane details

jokes.text: No, Bender. Robot hell is quite real. Here's our brochure.
jokes.setup: Bender thinking he's hallucinating robot hell
jokes.punchline: Hell having tourism brochures like a vacation destination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on hell as bureaucratic institution with marketing materials.

jokes.text: They shut it down after all those people... caught salmonella from the flume ride.
jokes.punchline: Absurd health violation that closed an amusement park
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about amusement park safety violations

jokes.text: Who would have thought hell would really exist? And that it would be in New Jersey?
jokes.punchline: The stereotypical New Jersey insult
jokes.explanation: Predictable New Jersey joke but delivered well within the context

jokes.text: Mind if I smoke?
jokes.setup: About to be tortured for smoking
jokes.punchline: Asking permission to do the thing he's being punished for
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - defiant even in hell with maximum economy

jokes.text: Wouldn't a solid gold fiddle weigh hundreds of pounds... and sound crummy? Well, it's mostly for show.
jokes.punchline: Logical problems with gold instrument, devil's casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Great logical deconstruction of the classic prize with perfect deadpan response

jokes.text: You'll only win a smaller silver fiddle. Also, I guess I'll kill one of you... Him.
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of murder as afterthought, pointing at Fry
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and the casual way he selects Fry is perfect

jokes.text: Well, we're boned.
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact assessment of their doom
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender catchphrase delivered at perfect moment with maximum economy

jokes.text: Stop them! They cheated!
jokes.punchline: The devil complaining about cheating
jokes.explanation: Good irony of the devil accusing others of cheating

jokes.text: I could if you dropped the stupid gold violin. Oh, sorry.
jokes.punchline: Fry holding onto the useless prize while they flee for their lives
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's misplaced priorities

jokes.text: I could if you dropped the stupid gold violin.
jokes.setup: Friends telling Bender to hurry while escaping
jokes.punchline: Bender's priorities still focused on the valuable but impractical prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bender's greed overriding practical concerns even while escaping hell.

jokes.text: Uh, do you think you could be... just a little less evil than that? I don't know. Do you think you could survive a 700-foot fall?
jokes.setup: Request for Bender to be less evil
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate threat of violence in response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - answering reasonable request with death threat, excellent character consistency

tmdb_overview: During a Beastie Boys concert, Bender runs into a high school buddy, Fender. He invites him to a party, which turns out to be robots getting high off of electricity, or "jacking on". After one try, Bender is hooked.
